zh.Paston, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可柏斯頓

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas de la Barre於大約1305年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可柏斯頓時，他的父親Thomas de la Barre28歲，母親Mrs. Thomas de la Barre25歲。他於大約1332年與Hawise Pembridge在英國賀福爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1388年6月在英國賀福爾享年84歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas de la Barre于大约1305年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可柏斯頓时，他的父亲Thomas de la Barre28岁，母亲Mrs. Thomas de la Barre25岁。他于大约1332年与Hawise Pembridge在英国賀福爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1388年6月在英国賀福爾享年84岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Barre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Anatole, Emile, Pierre, Alphonse, Andre, Cecile, Henri, Herve, Jacques, Marcel, Regean, Yves.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a gateway or barrier, or in a house encircled by a fence, from Old French barre ‘bar, obstruction’ (of obscure origin, possibly akin to the Celtic element barr ‘height’); or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, e.g. Barre-en-Ouche in Eure or Barre-de-Semilly in Manche. Compare Labarre .
French: probably also a habitational name from Barre in Lozère, named with the Celtic element barr ‘height’.

variants.name: Labarre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Alain, Andre, Armand, Aurore, Fernande, Gilles, Herve, Laure, Michel, Urbain.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a gateway or barrier, or in a house encircled by a fence, from Old French barre ‘bar, obstruction’ (see Barre 1), with fused feminine definite article la; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, e.g. Barre-en-Ouche in Eure or Barre-de-Semilly in Manche. Compare Labar , Labare , Labarr , Labor , Labore , Lebar , and Lebarre .
History: In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Jacques Genest or Genet dit Labarre from France (see Genest ).

variants.name: Baray
meanings: Hispanic (Mexico): unexplained.
In some cases possibly also French: variant of Barray, itself a variant of Barrey, a derivative of Barre 1. In France, it is found mainly in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Barrere
meanings: French (Barrère): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or barrier, or a metonymic occupational name for a gatekeeper, from a derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1); or a habitational name from (La) Barrère, the name of several places in the southwestern part of France.
English: variant of Barrer, for someone who lived by a town or castle gate, from Middle English barre ‘barrier, gateway’. Compare above and Barr 4.

variants.name: Barriere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Gaston, Monique, Philippe.

French (Barrière): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or barrier, or a metonymic occupational name for a gatekeeper, from a derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1); or a habitational name from (La) Barrière, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Gates .

variants.name: Barreau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Fresnel, Gabrielle, Jean-Louis, Jean-Marie, Jeanty, Michel, Michelene, Pierre.
French:

derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1), hence a nickname for a fence keeper or a topographic name for someone who lived in a house encircled by a fence.

variants.name: Barrier
meanings: French: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Old French barier, an agent derivative of bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1).
Altered form of French   Barriere  .
Altered form of German   Berger  .

variants.name: Barra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Marina, Antonio, Augusto, Biagio, Carlo, Domenic, Flavio, Gennaro, Lorenzo, Luciano, Rocco, Salvatore.  Spanish Jorge, Carlos, Diego, Enrique, Jaime, Juan, Manuel.
Italian (southern), Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name from barra ‘bar, rod’ (in the sense of ‘barrier, sandbank’), or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word.
French (southern): dialect variant of   Barre   1.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas de la Barre was born about 1305, in Paston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas de la Barre, was 28 and his mother, Mrs. Thomas de la Barre, was 25. He married Hawise Pembridge about 1332, in Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in June 1388, in Herefordshire, England, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas de la Barre est né environ 1305, à Paston, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas de la Barre, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Thomas de la Barre, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Hawise Pembridge environ 1332, à Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en juin 1388, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas de la Barre um 1305 in Paston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de la Barre 28 und seine Mutter Mrs. Thomas de la Barre 25. Er hat um 1332 in Herefordshire, England Hawise Pembridge geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb im Juni 1388 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Herefordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas de la Barre è nato il circa 1305 nel Paston, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Thomas de la Barre, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Thomas de la Barre, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Hawise Pembridge circa 1332, nel Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel giugno 1388, nel Herefordshire, England, all'età di 84 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de la Barreが約1305年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクプアストンで生まれた時，父Thomas de la Barreは28歳，母Mrs. Thomas de la Barreは25歳であった。約1332年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでHawise Pembridgeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1388年6月にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで84歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de la Barre(이)가 대략 1305년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 파스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de la Barre(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Thomas de la Barre(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1332년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 Hawise Pembridge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1388년 6월 영국 허퍼드실르에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas de la Barre nasceu aproximadamente 1305, em Paston, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas de la Barre, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Thomas de la Barre, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Hawise Pembridge aproximadamente 1332, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em junho de 1388, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 84 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas de la Barre родился около 1305 г. в местности пастон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas de la Barre было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Thomas de la Barre, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Hawise Pembridge около 1332 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер июнь 1388 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 84 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas de la Barre nació aproximadamente 1305, en Paston, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas de la Barre, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Mrs. Thomas de la Barre, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Hawise Pembridge aproximadamente 1332, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en junio de 1388, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 84 años.
